mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal an ongoing labor market contraction that erodes household income and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premia rise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has declined every month from January 2008 through July 2008
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following months with negative PAYEMS prints are positive in more than 55% of instances, or rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows including 2008-2009.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already near cyclical lows (below 2.5%) while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains elevated above 5%, the Fed is constrained from easing further to support growth, and this policy paralysis triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as investors price stagflation risk.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.01% in July 2008 while CPIAUCSL index near 219 reflecting elevated YoY inflation from oil price shock
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of periods meeting the trigger, or the effect disappears once CPI YoY inflation is computed and falls below 5% threshold within the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained downtrend in housing starts (HOUST) below prior cyclical peaks signals deteriorating residential investment demand that precedes broader consumption and credit contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, with the effect strongest during active housing downturns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from 1066K to 1046K vintage-to-revised readings in mid-2008, continuing a multi-year decline from 2005-06 peaks
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant negative skew relative to baseline during periods of HOUST decline exceeding 20% from 12-month prior peak, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during housing downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deterioration in the unemployment rate (UNRATE rising) and industrial production (INDPRO falling) jointly confirms a broad-based recessionary regime rather than a sector-specific slowdown, and this joint signal produces stronger negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp over prior 3 months AND INDPRO declines on a 3-month annualized basis
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose to 5.7-5.8% by mid-2008 while INDPRO vintage readings show weakening industrial activity
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal underperforms the single-series UNRATE-only signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or 20-day forward returns following joint triggers are not significantly more negative than returns following UNRATE-only triggers (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Short-run momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real-time manufacturing and supply-side activity that is quickly priced by equity markets; a positive month-over-month INDPRO print predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as it confirms near-term growth resilience.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive INDPRO month-over-month prints are not significantly higher than returns following negative prints (t-test p > 0.05), or effect reverses sign in more than one 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth crosses from positive to negative on a year-over-year basis, it signals a consumer spending contraction that has historically preceded broader earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value of 384,631M in July 2008 later revised down to 367,594M, indicating deteriorating and overstated retail momentum
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where RSAFS YoY growth first turns negative, or the signal shows no statistically significant negative mean return relative to unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated (YoY > 4.5%) and FEDFUNDS is simultaneously near multi-year lows, the interaction signals the market perceives the central bank as behind the curve on inflation control, amplifying volatility and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter that exceed what either series predicts independently.
evidence_refs: fact:mid-2008 combination of CPI near 219 (elevated YoY inflation from energy prices) with FEDFUNDS at 2.01% reflects a policy-inflation mismatch
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during joint-trigger periods are not significantly more negative than returns during periods when only the CPI threshold or only the FEDFUNDS threshold is met individually (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-like threshold crossing, where the 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises 0.3pp or more above its prior 12-month low, marks the recognized onset of recession and triggers an immediate negative repricing of equities over the next 5 trading days as algorithmic and discretionary traders react to the confirmed recession signal.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose from a 2007 low near 4.4% to 5.7-5.8% by mid-2008, approaching Sahm-rule recession threshold levels
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following the threshold crossing are positive in more than 50% of historical instances, or the signal fails to produce statistically significant negative mean returns relative to the unconditional 5-day baseline (p > 0.05).
